summary: WeCom Agent now has a ten-page Sourcey documentation site grounded in its maintained MIT-licensed Python repository, with 38 public APIs documented, a sealed runx Sourcey receipt, a dedicated Read the Docs project, and repository README adoption.

sourcey.deployment_method: Read the Docs custom build from the connected GitHub repository using .readthedocs.yaml

coverage.notes: The reference covers the public CLI foundation, normal documents, local document fallback, standard online sheets, smart sheets, and local file utilities. Workflow pages add real examples, confirmation rules, permission limits, and troubleshooting guidance.

observations.evidence: All 11 Sourcey graph steps succeeded: discover, approve, author, write, build, critique, revise, rebuild, verify, and package.
runx verify reported one valid tree with 12 receipts and no findings.
Repository LICENSE at the pinned source commit.

observations.result: 10 of 10 pages and 6 core assets returned HTTP 200
observations.evidence: Stranger-reachable verification against wecom-agent.readthedocs.io after Read the Docs build 33627849.

observations.result: 38 source entries and 38 rendered API headings

observations.evidence: All 12 unique relative href/src targets across the live generated HTML set returned HTTP 200.
Sourcey build icon validation against Heroicons v2 outline.
The dedicated wecom-agent.readthedocs.io project is connected to tzwkb/wecom-agent, builds main through the committed .readthedocs.yaml, and is linked from both project READMEs.
